name: steel-string-e2
description: Plucked steel string, E2. Its defining physics are real and measurable, not decorative. Partials follow the stiff-string dispersion law f_n = n*f0*sqrt(1 + B*n^2) with f0 = 82.41 Hz and inharmonicity coefficient B = 2e-4 (steel guitar strings sit around 1e-4..5e-4): the top partials ride progressively sharp of the harmonic series, which is what gives a steel string its shimmer and is why it will never tune perfectly against a pure-harmonic reference. T60 falls with partial number (higher partials shed energy faster), and the strike/pickup maps are the ideal-string shape phi_m(x) = sin(m*pi*x): plucking or picking up at a partial's node mutes that partial, so where the pickup sits combs the spectrum (a bridge pickup sounds brighter than a neck pickup). Both are checkable against a render: measure_inharmonicity recovers B, and a pickup moved onto a partial's node attenuates it.
